title: Complete DDD application layer and port boundaries
summary: TASK-30 acceptance met and verified: application/ports/adapters split in place, Pi imports isolated, deprecated package scope blocked, core/engine folders deleted, runtime import and live codewiki_state pass.

goal.outcome: Core CodeWiki workflows are callable without Pi-specific types through stable application-layer use cases.
acceptance: Application layer exposes use cases for read state, mutate task, manage session, run heartbeat, setup/bootstrap, and rebuild where appropriate.
Ports cover filesystem, project resolution, notification, session access, locking, message delivery, git/subprocess/rebuild needs without importing Pi types.
Domain/application modules do not import `@earendil-works/*`, deprecated `@mariozechner/*`, or Pi UI types.
Existing Pi tools and commands continue to work through adapters or compatibility wrappers.
No `core/**` or `engine/**` source folders remain.
Native Node TS runtime import of the extension entrypoint and rebuild infrastructure succeeds.
non_goals: Do not add MCP/Codex/Claude adapters in this task.
verification: Exercise Pi tool wrappers through smoke coverage or targeted tests.

delta.desired: Application use cases form the stable agent-agnostic API that future adapters can call.
delta.current: Application/domain/infrastructure/adapters layout is in place; core/** and engine/** are removed; Pi adapter wraps tools/commands/UI; architecture guard blocks deprecated package scope and transitional folders; runtime TS imports use explicit specifiers.
delta.closure: Use cases exist with green tests, runtime imports, and Pi compatibility preserved.

title: Enforce graph-backed traceability and active-roadmap policy
summary: TASK-035 acceptance met: sanitized stale roadmap code_paths; enriched untethered KB docs with code_paths; added unscoped-doc and evidence-link lint coverage; active roadmap queue no longer includes archived tasks.

goal.outcome: Agents can reliably trace intended knowledge to roadmap delta to code ownership through enforced metadata and generated views.
acceptance: Knowledge frontmatter requirements are documented and enforced for ids, summaries, owners, and relevant code/spec links.
Roadmap tasks validate spec_paths, code_paths, goals, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and evidence links.
Lint/reporting identifies missing or broken horizontal and vertical mappings.
Generated views expose compact navigation packets for this mapping without becoming canonical truth.
non_goals: Do not require every code file to have a one-to-one doc.
Do not make roadmap tasks duplicate all knowledge content.
verification: Run a rebuild and confirm codewiki_state refresh=true passes.
Add/adjust smoke fixtures for invalid mapping cases.

delta.desired: Lint and graph catch broken cross-layer references; knowledge frontmatter is consistently present and validated.
delta.current: Lint already checks missing fields, broken links, and missing code paths. Graph already exposes reconciliation items. Frontmatter is partially validated. Stale spec_paths/code_paths on some roadmap tasks cause lint warnings. Knowledge docs need consistent frontmatter audit.
delta.closure: Lint validates cross-layer mapping and evidence links; generated roadmap queue excludes archived tasks; task close validation passes.

title: Automate implementation-build handoff and publication prep
summary: TASK-043 closed: implementation_build handoff/publication prep implemented, validated, and smoke-tested.

goal.outcome: After implementation work, CodeWiki records evidence and compact handoff context that can drive closure, resume, commit/PR/issue/release drafts, and push-readiness checks under validation/policy gates.
acceptance: Implementation builds include task/work refs, docs/spec refs, files changed, checks run, acceptance mapping, validation refs, risks, and publication draft fields when useful.
Task closure or checkpoint can persist next focus and compact resume context without relying on chat transcript memory.
Commit, push, release, and remote issue updates are prepared as recommendations and require explicit validation/policy approval before execution.
Pi/VCC/native compaction integration points are isolated in adapters or documented with safe fallbacks.
Smoke or characterization coverage proves resume after handoff uses persisted compact context rather than stale chat state.
non_goals: Do not make automatic remote push the default behavior.
Do not replace builds, roadmap, or validation with chat transcript summaries.
Do not couple core CodeWiki semantics to Pi-only compaction APIs.
verification: Create implementation_build for a documentation-only or small code task
Validate publication-ready fields without executing remote updates
npm test if runtime code changes.

delta.desired: Implementation evidence is the handoff source for closure, resume, and publication prep.
delta.current: Implementation builds now include task snapshot, linked refs, compact resume handoff, validation/risk refs, and recommendation-only publication drafts guarded by approval policy.
delta.closure: TASK-043 implementation evidence recorded in .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-10-task-043-handoff-publication-prep.json; checks pass.

title: Support tester and builder roles from documentation builds
summary: TASK-046 closed: optional tester/builder split supported from documentation builds and roadmap items with separated implementation evidence.

goal.outcome: The implementation loop can optionally split tester and builder roles while preserving documentation_build, roadmap, validation, and implementation_build truth boundaries.
acceptance: Implementation workflow can request a tester role from a documentation_build and roadmap work item before code changes.
Tester output focuses on tests or test-design evidence and avoids implementation changes where practical.
Builder consumes documentation_build, roadmap item, and tests, then produces implementation evidence after checks pass.
Implementation_build distinguishes test-design evidence, code-change evidence, checks, acceptance mapping, and validation refs.
Validation gateway can review tester/builder split without requiring separate agents for every task.
non_goals: Do not always require separate agents for every task.
Do not implement a generic multi-agent framework.
Do not revive legacy duplicated requirement artifacts as requirements briefs.
verification: Update skill/tool docs for tester/builder inputs and outputs
Run stale grep for roadmap-work-item terminology
Exercise workflow on a small task or dry-run with documented evidence mapping.

delta.desired: Tester/builder roles consume documentation builds and roadmap items with separated implementation evidence.
delta.current: Implementation builds accept/emits tester evidence, builder evidence, checks, acceptance mapping, validation refs, and handoff context. Skills and KB route implementation work through documentation_build + roadmap item, not duplicated requirements artifacts.
delta.closure: TASK-046 implementation evidence recorded in .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-10-task-046-tester-builder-evidence.json; checks and stale-grep pass.

title: Align CodeWiki loop skills with compiler lexicon
summary: TASK-051 closed: primary CodeWiki loop skills now match compiler lexicon exactly with no deprecated aliases.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki packaged skills guide the three canonical loops with names and descriptions that match the lexicon: feedback, documentation, and implementation.
acceptance: Primary loop skills are named `codewiki-feedback`, `codewiki-documentation`, and `codewiki-implementation`.
The `codewiki` router skill routes to the three loop skills and labels validation/research/architecture/view-audit as gateway/support skills.
Skill frontmatter descriptions use loop terms as activation triggers and avoid generic `plan`/`task` vocabulary for primary loops.
README, package smoke tests, KB code_paths, and related skill references are updated.
Old the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name skill directories and references are removed, not aliased or deprecated.
non_goals: Do not change loop semantics or build schemas.
Do not remove validation/research/architecture/view-audit support workflows.
Do not make skills the source of truth.
Do not keep backward-compatible aliases for old loop skill names.
verification: Run grep for stale the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name references in live docs/source/tests/roadmap.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm graph maps the new skill paths.

delta.desired: Packaged skill names and descriptions mirror the canonical compiler loops with no deprecated aliases.
delta.current: Primary skills were renamed to `codewiki-documentation` and `codewiki-implementation`; old directories removed; router/README/tests/KB/source refs updated.
delta.closure: Pending final implementation build, validation, and close.

title: Add scoped change claims for parallel work
summary: TASK-052 closed: scoped change claims implemented for parallel docs/roadmap/build/validation/code coordination, with graph/status/state exposure and smoke coverage.

goal.outcome: Parallel CodeWiki sessions can claim arbitrary change scopes and see cross-layer overlap warnings/conflicts before making semantic changes.
acceptance: A first-class claim/lease model exists for session-owned change scopes with optional roadmap task/build anchors.
Claims support layers for knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, validation, and graph/source paths.
A Pi-facing tool can create, release, heartbeat, and list active claims without editing generated graph files directly.
Generated state/index derives active claims and overlap conflicts, distinguishing read/read, read/write warning, and write/write conflict.
Status/graph surfaces active claim count, conflicts, and per-session claim details alongside existing task-session parallel state.
Docs and skills clarify roadmap tasks remain work truth while claims are temporary concurrency leases, not source of truth.
Smoke coverage proves claim creation, release/expiry, and overlap detection.
Roadmap id generation does not reuse archived task ids.
non_goals: Do not implement distributed cross-machine locks in this first slice.
Do not make claims replace roadmap tasks, compiler builds, validation reports, git, or code review.
Do not block every write automatically before explicit claim enforcement exists.
verification: Run direct current-source graph rebuild and inspect claims view.
Run codewiki_state refresh=false after current-source rebuild.

delta.desired: Parallel sessions coordinate any scoped change through temporary claims while durable truth stays in KB/roadmap/builds/validation/code.
delta.current: `codewiki_claim` claim/release/heartbeat/list is implemented; `.codewiki/claims.json` stores lease state; graph/status derive claim counts and overlaps; docs/skills updated; archived id reuse guard added.
delta.closure: Pending implementation build, validation, and task close.

title: Move scoped claims to runtime storage
summary: TASK-053 closed: scoped claim store moved to gitignored runtime storage; graph/status/state remain derived views only.

goal.outcome: Scoped claims are stored as ephemeral runtime input while graph/status remain derived read-only views.
acceptance: Claim persistence path is under `.codewiki/runtime/claims.json` or equivalent explicit runtime location.
Docs/config/templates/gitignore stop presenting top-level `.codewiki/claims.json` as state truth.
Graph/status/state still derive claim views from runtime claim input.
Smoke tests use the runtime claim path and still cover release/expiry/overlap behavior.
non_goals: Do not store claims only in generated graph.
Do not keep a backwards-compatible alias for the old top-level claim path.

delta.desired: Claim store is clearly ephemeral runtime input; graph contains only derived claim view.
delta.current: Claims now use `.codewiki/runtime/claims.json`; graph/status/state derive claim views; old top-level claim path is absent from active docs/source/tests/config.
delta.closure: Validation pass recorded; ready to close.

title: Fix reconciliation lifecycle consumption
summary: Implemented reconciliation lifecycle consumption. Graph now consumes accepted feedback/documentation builds when downstream documentation, roadmap changes, implementation links, validation source(s), or pass verdict evidence references them. Historical absorbed handoffs are covered by drift-audit validation. Tests and docs updated.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki status reports reconciliation drift only for real unconsumed handoffs, not for completed historical builds.
acceptance: Accepted feedback builds with linked documentation builds no longer produce documentation-loop drift.
Accepted documentation builds with linked roadmap/implementation evidence no longer produce roadmap/documentation drift.
Validated or accepted implementation evidence closes the corresponding reconciliation route.
codewiki_state after refresh no longer reports the current false reconciliation warning for already completed work.
Tests cover consumed feedback, documentation, and implementation build reconciliation behavior.
non_goals: Add a new compiler loop.
Persist reconciliation truth outside canonical sources.
Recreate removed queue files or deprecated aliases.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows no false reconciliation drift for completed builds.

delta.desired: Graph reconciliation reflects real unconsumed build handoffs and routes only actionable work.
delta.current: Graph reports 18 reconciliation drifts from accepted feedback/documentation builds even though downstream docs/tasks/implementations exist.
delta.closure: Update lifecycle/reconciliation logic, add tests, refresh graph, and record implementation evidence.

title: Implement Build Contract v2 and closure briefs
summary: Implemented Build Contract v2. Feedback builds now persist and require approved diff_table rows, implementation builds require closure_brief, writers emit explicit consumes/produces DAG fields, graph reconciliation consumes those edges, and v2 lint/tests enforce the contract while historical builds remain legacy.

goal.outcome: New builds are compact, structured, and trustworthy transition points that preserve user intent and prove task closure through all layers.
acceptance: `codewiki_build` accepts and persists feedback diff-table rows with current state, desired state, rationale, affected layers, risk, and user action.
New accepted feedback builds require approved diff rows and expose them as canonical intent contract data.

`codewiki_build kind=implementation` accepts and persists a compact closure_brief with user intent, implemented changes, layers updated, acceptance evidence, checks, non-goals preserved, and remaining risks.
New builds expose explicit `consumes` and `produces` fields; graph reconciliation prefers those edges over legacy inference.
Lint/schema/tests flag missing v2 required fields for new builds while treating historical builds as legacy cold artifacts.
Task-close or implementation summary can show the closure brief so users can trust their intent was implemented through knowledge, roadmap, code/tests, checks, and validation.
non_goals: Do not migrate every historical build immediately.
Do not implement the interactive diff-table UI in this task.
Do not make pending unapproved diff rows canonical truth.
verification: Create smoke feedback/documentation/implementation builds using v2 fields and confirm graph edges/closure brief output.

delta.desired: Build artifacts are concise v2 DAG handoffs with mandatory user-facing intent and closure contracts.
delta.current: Builds are mixed v1 structures; feedback diff tables and implementation closure briefs are not enforced by schema or writer code.
delta.closure: Build schema/writer/lint/graph/tests enforce v2 while preserving historical legacy reads.

title: Plan Efficiency Foundation sprint
summary: Implemented Efficiency Foundation: schema v4 config, sprint cohorts, workflow cursor, scope views, scoped agency budgets/session plan, hot/warm/cold/purge GC views, and quiet rebuild defaults. SPRINT-001 marked closed.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can scope hot state and agency by roadmap, sprint, or task with explicit budgets, safe parallelism, workflow cursor state, and garbage-collection checkpoints.
acceptance: Config schema v4 documents and loads budgets, agency scopes, parallelism, GC policy, and quiet rebuild defaults.
Roadmap supports a minimal `roadmap -> sprint -> task` cohort model, and graph exposes roadmap/sprint/task scope views.
Runtime workflow cursor records active loop, expected output, scope, and exit gate, while graph exposes it as a derived view.
Build DAG uses explicit `consumes` and `produces` edges across feedback, documentation, implementation, validation, roadmap, and code.
GC/checkpoint policy classifies artifacts as hot, warm, cold, or purgeable and includes sprint-close hooks for consumed builds, pass validations, closed task shards, and expired runtime state.
Agency can plan runs scoped to roadmap, sprint, or task with token, time, cost, write, session, and risk budgets; session-per-sprint spawning is gated by confirmed harness capability and claims.
non_goals: Do not build the visual UI in this sprint.
Do not implement unsafe unbounded autonomous execution.
Do not require all existing flat-roadmap users to manually migrate before compatibility behavior exists.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows scoped hot-state views and no false reconciliation drift.

delta.desired: Efficiency foundation sprint exists as planned roadmap delta with explicit scope, budget, DAG, cursor, and GC outcomes.
delta.current: Agency has triggers/budgets but no real sprint model, workflow cursor, token/cost budget policy, hot/cold GC, or session-per-sprint spawning policy.
delta.closure: After Build Contract v2 lands, implement this sprint as the next scoped foundation.

title: Plan Status, Graph, and Diff Table UI sprint
summary: Implemented Status, Graph, and Diff Table UI sprint: Home shows scope/budget/claims/GC factors, Graph tab shows scoped graph and build DAG rows, Diff tab edits pending runtime diff rows and falls back to accepted feedback rows, codewiki_diff_table creates pending rows, and /wiki-status can open graph/diff sections. SPRINT-002 marked closed.

goal.outcome: Users can visually inspect health, scope, graph relationships, and approve/reject/defer feedback diff rows without reading generated JSON or relying on chat-only confirmations.
acceptance: Status panel shows compact health, active scope, sprint/task focus, gates, budget burn, claims/conflicts, and next action.
Graph navigation exposes source-of-truth links, build DAG edges, stale/missing links, and scoped roadmap/sprint/task graph slices.
Interactive diff-table UI supports approve, reject, defer, and propose-alternative actions per row before accepted rows compile into feedback builds.
UI reads graph/API state and runtime pending diff tables instead of duplicating canonical truth.
User-facing closure brief is visible from task close/implementation evidence.
non_goals: Do not implement before Build Contract v2 and core scope contracts exist.
Do not make UI-only state canonical truth.
Do not duplicate full generated JSON in the panel.
verification: Graph/status panel fixtures or snapshots for core states.

delta.desired: UI sprint is planned but deferred until data contracts and scoped hot-state foundation exist.
delta.current: Status panel and graph navigation exist as product expectations and partial Pi UI, but diff-table interactions and scoped graph visualization are not implemented.
delta.closure: Run after Build Contract v2 and Efficiency Foundation provide stable backing data.
